catalog_metadata.title: Simpson Strong-Tie Structural Connector Catalog
catalog_metadata.address: 5956 West Las Positas Blvd, Pleasanton, CA 94588
catalog_metadata.note: Current catalog is C-C-2026 (2026-2027). C-C-2024 replaced by C-C-2026 effective 2026.

joist_hangers.description: Face-mount and top-flange hangers connecting joists, beams, and rafters to supporting members

LUS.full_name: LUS Light-Capacity U-Shaped Hanger with Double-Shear Nailing
LUS.description: Economical face-mount hanger for common lumber sizes with double-shear nailing for greater strength with fewer nails
LUS.finish: G90 Galvanized; ZMAX® available (Z suffix); Stainless Steel available (SS suffix)
LUS.application: Face-mount single and multi-ply joists to headers, beams, ledgers
installation_notes: Double-shear nailing: nails driven at angle through joist into header
With 2x carrying members: 0.162x3.5 nails into header reduce load to 0.64; 0.148x3 nails reduce load to 0.77
With 3x carrying members: no load reduction with correct nail sizes
Cannot be modified

allowable_loads_with_sd_screws.note: SD Connector Screws (SD9112 = #9 x 1-1/2"; SD9212 = #9 x 2-1/2")

HUS.full_name: HUS Heavy U-Shaped Hanger with Double-Shear Nailing
HUS.description: Heavy-duty face-mount hanger for higher loads; double-shear nailing; cannot be used with I-joists
HUS.finish: G90 Galvanized; ZMAX available; Stainless Steel (SS suffix) for select models
HUS.application: Face-mount higher-load joists, beams; NOT for I-joists
installation_notes: Cannot be modified
Not designed for welded or nailer applications
With 2x carrying members: load reductions apply (0.64 or 0.77 depending on nail size)
Joist nails must be driven at angle through joist into header

gap_notes.max_gap_with_reduced_load: 3/8 inch (use Table in tech bulletin for reduction factors)
gap_notes.htv_option_for_gap_to_half_inch: HTU series hangers rated for up to 1/2-inch gap

HHUS_HGUS.description: Heavy-duty hangers with skewable and/or slopeable seat; HHUS can skew AND slope, HGUS skews only

skew_adjustment.HHUS_skew_only: Max 45° skew; load = 0.85 of table load
skew_adjustment.HHUS_slope_only_or_sloped_and_skewed: Max 45°; download = 0.65 of table load; uplift = 0.72 of table load, max 2475 lb
skew_adjustment.HGUS_skew_only: Max 45° skew; load reduction per table

GLT_GLB.full_name: GLT/GLS/GLB - Glulam and Engineered Lumber Hangers
GLT_GLB.description: Hangers designed for glulam beams and engineered lumber with higher load capacity
GLT_GLB.catalog_pages: C-C-2024 pages referenced in product index
GLT_GLB.note: GLB is a Beam Seat connector for masonry applications (ESR-2877)

hurricane_ties.description: Ties connecting rafters/trusses to wall plates to resist wind uplift and lateral forces

H1A.description: Next-generation U-shaped hurricane tie for nominal 2x lumber; connects rafter/truss to wall plate; installs on both sides for high lateral resistance
H1A.application: Rafter/truss-to-top-plate connection for wind and seismic resistance
installation_notes: Hurricane ties do not replace solid blocking
Can be installed inside or outside wall
Flanges can face inward or outward
Do not fasten through truss plates from behind

fasteners.nails: (4) 0.131 x 1-1/2" to rafter/truss and (4) 0.131 x 1-1/2" to plates
fasteners.sd_screws: (4) SD9112 to rafter/truss, (4) SD9112 to plates

H2_5A.description: Single-sided hurricane tie for moderate loads; symmetrical design eliminates right/left parts; embossments in bend line strengthen connection
H2_5A.fasteners: (5) 0.131 x 1-1/2" nails to rafter, (5) 0.131 x 1-1/2" nails to plates

H10.description: Higher-capacity single-sided hurricane tie; installed on top chord of truss to wall plate
H10.note: H10A = previous version (1040 lb DF/SP uplift); current H10 is updated version

HM.description: Hurricane tie for metal plate connected trusses

H1_81Z.description: Hurricane tie for 1-3/4" wide engineered lumber

straps_and_ties.description: Flat metal straps and ties for tension connections, floor-to-floor continuity, and shearwall restraint

LSTA.description: Light-gauge precut strap for wind/seismic tension loads; staggered nail pattern minimizes splitting; floor-to-floor clear span
LSTA.application: Tension loads in wood-to-wood; edge of 2x members

MSTA.description: Medium-gauge precut strap for higher tension loads; staggered nail pattern; floor-to-floor clear span
MSTA.finish: G90 Galvanized; ZMAX (Z suffix); Stainless Steel (SS suffix)

CMST.description: Continuous coiled strap cut to length on jobsite; resists tension and uplift loads; double 2x stud or post application; ideal for roof uplift and shearwall overturning

models.note: C = countersunk nail slots for lower profile

installation_notes: Cut length = 2x End Length + clear span dimension
Use only every other round hole if wood tends to split
Lap splicing allowed per table; 0.148x2.5 nails only in splice
CMSTC uses 0.148x3-1/4 sinker nails

CS.description: Light coiled strap for general tension applications

MSTI.description: Interior medium strap tie; catalog pages C-C-2024 pp. 278-279

MST.description: General medium strap tie for wood-to-wood connections

post_bases.description: Connectors attaching wood posts to concrete foundations with uplift resistance and 1-inch standoff

ABU.full_name: ABU Adjustable Post Base with Uplift (High Capacity)
ABU.description: High-capacity adjustable post base; 1-inch standoff; slotted anchor hole for adjustability; nails, SD screws or bolts
installation_notes: 1-inch standoff meets code for posts in basements or weather-exposed
Slotted base for post placement adjustment around anchor bolt
Post bases not recommended for non-top-supported installations
ABU88Z/1010Z/1212Z: may use SDS screws for same table load

ABA.description: Standard-capacity adjustable post base; 1-inch standoff; nails or SD screws (no bolts)

ABW.full_name: ABW Adjustable Post Base with Wind Uplift (Medium Capacity)
ABW.description: Medium-capacity adjustable post base; three-sided nailing before bending fourth flange

CBS_CBSQ.full_name: CBS/CBSQ Column Base (Being replaced by CBST/CBSTQ)
CBS_CBSQ.description: Column base for 4x4, 4x6, 6x6 posts; concealed design; superseded by CBST series per engineering letter L-C-CBSTQZ26
CBS_CBSQ.note: As of 2026, CBST and CBSTQ replace CBS and CBSQ series

CB.description: Adjustable column base for wood posts on concrete; includes various post sizes

seismic_loads.sdc_a_b: Wind and SDC A&B loads apply

EPB.description: Post base with elevated standoff for posts on concrete; catalog page C-C-2024 p. 74

post_caps.description: Post caps connecting beams to tops of posts with uplift and lateral resistance

BC.description: Light-duty 18-gauge post cap connecting beam to post; provides uplift and lateral resistance; opposing flanges increase lateral resistance
BC.note: BC4 uplift reduced from 980 lb to 605 lb after 2019 ASTM D7147 update

AC.description: Adjustable post cap; catalog pages C-C-2024 pp. 88-89

CC_CCQ.description: Heavy-duty column caps for larger posts and beams; catalog pp. 94-97
CC_CCQ.note: CCQ/ECCQ lateral loads require beam to be laterally supported by other means

ACHZ.description: New in C-C-2024; medium- to high-capacity post cap filling gap between light-duty BC and heavy-duty CCQ

angles_and_plates.description: Framing angles, gusset angles, and lateral tie plates for general wood framing connections

A35.description: Balanced, completely reversible framing angle with exclusive bend slot for instant field bends; speed prong for temporary hold; 18 gauge
A35.fasteners: (9) or (12) 0.131x1-1/2" nails (depending on connection type); SD9112 screws approved
special_notes: Can be used over 1/2" wood structural panel sheathing with 0.131x1-1/2 nails, no load reduction
When installed on each side of joist, minimum joist thickness = 3"
A35SS stainless loads may be lower than carbon steel—use SCNR ring-shank nails to match

A34.description: Similar to A35 but not reversible; speed prong for temporary hold; 18 gauge

A_series_general.description: A-series includes many angle sizes (A21, A22, A23, A24, A25, A26, A34, A35, A66, A6, A66Z)

LTP4_LTP5.description: Transfers shear forces for top plate-to-rim board or blocking connections; embossments enhance performance
LTP4_LTP5.irc_note: LTP4 satisfies IRC R602.10.6.4 continuously sheathed portal frame framing anchor requirements

HL.description: Heavy angle and gusset; catalog p. 303

L_angles.description: L-series reinforcing and skewable angles (L50, L70, L90 etc.); catalog p. 301
L_angles.note: L50, L70, L90 refer to angle leg dimensions in mm in some international catalogs; US catalog uses inch dimensions

holdowns.description: Holdowns anchor shearwall posts to concrete foundations resisting overturning forces

HDU.description: Screw holdown for shearwalls and braced-wall panels; SDS screws supplied with holdown; predeflected seat; no stud bolts needed
HDU.note: HDU series being discontinued and replaced by HDUE series (per L-C-HDUECONV25, 2025)
HDU.fasteners: 1/4" x 2-1/2" SDS Heavy-Duty Connector screws (supplied with holdown)

allowable_tension_loads_per_esr2330.note: Assembly loads include bolt, screws, and holdown. See ESR-2330 Table 1B for full table.

HDUE.description: New higher-capacity holdown replacing HDU series; models HDUE3 through HDUE17; listed in ESR-2330
HDUE.note: Released 2025; replaces HDU series. See engineering letter L-C-HDUECONV25.

HHDQ.description: Extra-high load holdown; direct sill plate installation; suitable for back-to-back applications; SDS screws supplied
HHDQ.finish: Simpson Strong-Tie gray paint; HHDQ11 available in stainless steel
HHDQ.compression_note: HHDQ installed horizontally achieves compression loads with standard nut on underside of load transfer plate

HDQ8.description: Unique seat design minimizes deflection; very high load capacity; SDS screws installed

STHD.description: Strap-style holdown for retrofit applications and where minimum post width is required

PHD.description: Plate holdown for shearwall applications; uses standard anchor bolts; per ESR-2330

DTT2.description: Deck tension tie and holdown connection for deck ledger-to-house lateral connections; per ESR-2330

anchors_and_bolts.description: Cast-in-place anchor bolts for holdowns, shearwalls, and post bases

SSTB.description: Proprietary cast-in-place anchor bolt optimized for holdowns and Strong-Wall shearwalls; offset S-bend provides mechanical interlock with concrete; first ICC-ES proprietary anchor bolt per AC 399
installation_notes: Suitable for monolithic and two-pour (4" slab) concrete
Minimum concrete compressive strength: 2,500 psi
Install before concrete pour using AnchorMate holders (ABL/ABS)
Stamped embedment line aids installation at correct depth
For HDG models: chase threads or use overtapped nuts (NUT5/8-OST, etc.)
Two-pour loads: SSTB20→use SSTB16 loads; SSTB24→SSTB20 loads; SSTB34/36→SSTB28 loads
CMU: requires horizontal #4 rebar in second course + vertical #4 rebar in adjacent cell

seismic_design_categories.sdc_c: Per IBC 1613, detached 1-2 story dwellings in SDC C may use Wind/SDC A&B loads
seismic_design_categories.sdc_c_f: Separate SDC C-F loads (lower); see tables

allowable_tension_loads_stemwall_lbs.note: Loads are ASD. For LRFD: seismic x1.43, wind x1.67. Midwall = 1.5le from end.

SB.description: Proprietary anchor bolt with mechanical end bearing plate; companion to SSTB in ESR-2611

DTT1Z_DTT2Z.description: Deck tension tie resisting lateral loads from deck to house structure; also used as holdown; ZMAX coated

shearwalls.description: Prefabricated shearwall panels for lateral force resistance in earthquakes and high winds

SSW_Steel_Strong_Wall.description: Prefabricated steel panel shearwall with highest allowable loads; easy installation; preattached wood studs; code listed
SSW_Steel_Strong_Wall.finish: Vertical panel: galvanized; top/base plates: Simpson gray paint
SSW_Steel_Strong_Wall.includes: Strong-Drive SDS screws, nuts, and installation instructions
SSW_Steel_Strong_Wall.naming_convention: SSW[width_in]X[nominal_height_ft] — e.g. SSW18X8 = 18" wide x 8 ft nominal
SSW_Steel_Strong_Wall.note: For heights 11 ft and above, thickness is 5.5" (uses 2x6 framing)

WSWH_Strong_Wall_Wood.description: Patented wood shearwall with highest allowable loads; field-trimmable; versatile for residential and commercial
WSWH_Strong_Wall_Wood.note: Load tables in Strong-Wall Shearwalls catalog; not in C-C-2024

Strong_Rod_Systems.description: Continuous rod tiedown system for shearwall overturning restraint and roof uplift restraint

fasteners.description: Structural screws and nails for connector installations and wood-to-wood connections

SDS.description: 1/4-inch diameter high-strength structural wood screw; no predrilling; Type-17 point; double-barrier coating; hex head with integrated washer; serrated threads; ideal for connector installations and wood-to-wood
SDS.head: 3/8" hex head with 0.500" integrated washer
SDS.material_standard: Carbon steel with double-barrier coating; Type 316 SS available
applications: Connector installations per approved code reports

SD.description: Nail replacement screw specifically designed for connector installations; approved for LUS, HUS, hurricane ties, angles, and many other connectors; single fastener load values exceed 10d/16d common nails
SD.note: Only approved SD screws may substitute for nails in specific connectors; verify connector listing before substituting

SDWH.description: High-strength hex-head timber screw for beam-to-pile and heavy timber connections; load rated
SDWH.note: SDWH27400G = 1/4" x 4" SDWH used for 1616HTQ heavy T-strap beam-to-pile connections

SDWC.description: Truss and connector screw; used with LUS hangers when installed with 0.148x1-1/2 nails (repair option)
SDWC.technical_note: L-LUSSDWC26 — adjustment factors for LUS with 0.148x1-1/2 nails + 4.5" SDWC screws

SCN_SCNR.description: SCN smooth-shank and SCNR ring-shank connector nails for structural connectors; stainless available

BP_BPS.description: BP/BPS Bearing Plates for column and beam bases; catalog pp. 48-49

CNW.description: CNW Coupler Nut for connecting threaded rod to anchor bolt; catalog p. 50

FJA_FSA.description: FJA/FSA Foundation Anchors for wood framing to concrete; catalog p. 27

FRFP.description: FRFP Retrofit Foundation Plate; catalog p. 26

FWANZ.description: FWANZ Foundation Wall Angle; catalog p. 28

DTC_STC.description: DTC/STC Roof Truss Clips; catalog p. 241

TBE.description: TBE Truss Bearing Enhancer; catalog pp. 237-238

GBC.description: GBC Gable Brace Connector; catalog pp. 242-243

DGT_DGHT.description: Two-hour fire-rated hangers for multifamily Type III residential construction; new in C-C-2024

DJTZ.description: DJTZ Deck Joist Tie; catalog p. 310

DPTZ.description: DPTZ Deck Post Tie; catalog p. 307

LSC.description: LSC Adjustable Stringer Connector; catalog pp. 308-309

FB_FBR.description: FB/FBR Fence Rail Bracket; catalog p. 305

RR.description: RR Ridge Rafter Connector; catalog p. 142

HCP.description: HCP Hip Corner Plate; catalog pp. 127, 170

CJTZ.description: CJTZ/HCJTZ Concealed Joist Tie; catalog p. 128

CBTZ.description: CBTZ Concealed Beam Tie; catalog p. 90

ACBH_CBH.description: ACBH/CBH Concealed Beam Hanger; catalog pp. 150-153; engineering letters for glulam end grain

EPCZ_CPT.description: EPCZ/CPTZ Concealed Post Tie; catalog pp. 72-73

outdoor_accents.description: Black powder-coat decorative structural connectors for outdoor structures
outdoor_accents.note: APRTC2, APRTA2, APRTR — Rigid Tie connectors for 2x lumber

DSP_RSP.description: DSP/RSP Double/Single Stud Plate Ties; catalog pp. 291-293

SSP.description: SSP Single Stud Plate; catalog pp. 291-293

icc_es_esr_reports.overview: Simpson Strong-Tie maintains 60+ ICC-ES ESR and IAPMO UES ER reports. Key reports are listed below.

primary_reports.title: Simpson Strong-Tie Joist Hangers and Framing Connectors
primary_reports.covers: LUS, HUS, HHUS, and related face-mount hangers

primary_reports.title: Simpson Strong-Tie Face-Mount Hangers
primary_reports.covers: LUS, HUS, HHUS, HGUS and related face-mount solid sawn and engineered lumber hangers

primary_reports.title: Simpson Strong-Tie Screw Hold-Down Connectors
primary_reports.covers: HDU, HDUE, HDQ8, HHDQ, DTT2, PHD holdowns

primary_reports.title: Simpson Strong-Tie SSTB and SB Cast-In-Place Anchor Bolts
primary_reports.covers: SSTB series and SB series anchor bolts; first proprietary anchor bolt per ICC-ES AC 399

primary_reports.title: Strong-Drive SDS Heavy-Duty Connector Screw and SD Connector Screw
primary_reports.covers: SDS (1/4" dia) and SD connector screws for structural applications

primary_reports.title: Simpson Strong-Tie Connectors Attaching Wood Members to Masonry
primary_reports.covers: GLB/HGLB beam seats, HGT girder tiedowns, and masonry connectors

primary_reports.title: Simpson Strong-Tie Seismic and Hurricane Ties
primary_reports.covers: H-series hurricane ties including H1A, H2.5A, H10

primary_reports.title: Simpson Strong-Tie Master Index Report
primary_reports.covers: Index of all Simpson Strong-Tie ICC-ES evaluation reports
primary_reports.note: Reference this to find applicable ESR for any Simpson product

primary_reports.title: Simpson Strong-Tie Bypass Clip Connectors for Cold-Formed Steel
primary_reports.covers: SCB, MSCB, SSB, SCS, SSC, SFC, SJC, DSSCB, FCB, MFCB, DSHS, RCKW series

primary_reports.title: Strong-Drive Shouldered Screws
primary_reports.covers: XLSH series shouldered screws for cold-formed steel connector applications

primary_reports.title: Simpson Strong-Tie PDPAT Powder-Actuated Fasteners
primary_reports.covers: Power-actuated fasteners for connecting cold-formed steel connectors to structural steel

seismic_design_categories.overview: Most Simpson connectors are evaluated for SDC A through F. Key distinctions:

sdc_definitions.SDC_A_B: Low seismic hazard; standard connector loads apply; wind controls in most cases
sdc_definitions.SDC_C: Moderate seismic; most connectors applicable; detached 1-2 story dwellings may use SDC A&B loads per IBC 1613
sdc_definitions.SDC_D_E_F: High seismic; special requirements; SDC C-F loads are lower; special inspection may be required

special_inspection.ibc_trigger: IBC Section 1704.2 (1704.1 in 2009/2006 IBC) for main seismic-force-resisting systems
special_inspection.irc_exception: Special inspection generally NOT required for IRC applications

products_rated_sdc_c_through_f: SSTB anchor bolts (separate SDC C-F load tables)
CB Column Bases (separate cracked/uncracked concrete loads for SDC C-F)

load_table_dual_columns.note: Many products show two load columns: 'Wind and SDC A&B' and 'SDC C-F' — always use appropriate column for project seismic design category

IRC_2021.portal_frames: Section R602.10.6.4 — Continuously sheathed portal frames
IRC_2021.engineered_design: Section R301.1.3 — when engineer submits design

AWC_NDS.fastener_design: NDS Chapter 11 — Dowel-Type Fasteners (nails, screws, bolts, lag screws)
AWC_NDS.acceptance_criteria: ICC-ES AC13 — Acceptance Criteria for Joist Hangers
AWC_NDS.ac155: ICC-ES AC155 — Acceptance Criteria for Hold-Down Connectors

AWC_SDPWS_2021.shearwall_design: Section 4.3 — Wood Structural Panel Shear Walls

G90.description: Zinc galvanized; 0.90 oz/ft² zinc; standard indoor/light outdoor use
G90.use_case: Interior or dry exterior applications; NOT for treated lumber contact

ZMAX.description: G185 zinc coating; 2-3x more zinc than G90; approved for ACQ, CA-C, CCA treated lumber contact
ZMAX.use_case: Exterior decks, fences, and applications in contact with preservative-treated lumber

HDG.description: Hot-Dip Galvanized; ASTM A153; 1.25 oz/ft² minimum; highest zinc coating
HDG.use_case: Severe marine or industrial environments; SSTB and selected holdowns

SS.description: Stainless Steel Type 304 or 316; highest corrosion resistance
SS.use_case: Marine/coastal; ACQ lumber with high chloride exposure; swimming pool areas

paint.description: Simpson Strong-Tie gray paint (no corrosion protection); indoor use only

treated_lumber_compatibility.ACQ_CA_C_MCA: Use ZMAX, HDG, or SS connectors; hot-dip galvanized nails (ASTM A153)
treated_lumber_compatibility.CCA: G90 may be acceptable at lower preservative levels; verify with lumber treater
treated_lumber_compatibility.fire_retardant: Contact Simpson or lumber treater for recommendations

installation_general_notes.fastener_selection: Always use listed fastener types and sizes; substitute screws only when explicitly listed in catalog or code report
installation_general_notes.nail_installation: Common nails per ASTM F1667; pneumatic nails per same standard; clipped-head nails may have lower lateral values
installation_general_notes.edge_distances: Follow NDS minimum edge/end distances; connector nail holes are pre-located for code compliance
installation_general_notes.load_combinations: ASD loads shown; do not combine with other duration increases unless specifically noted
contractor_notes: Install all specified fasteners in all holes unless fewer nails option shown in table
Do not over-drive nails — flush with connector surface
For hurricane ties: do not fasten through truss plates from behind
Post bases: do not use for non-top-supported (fences, unbraced carports)
installation_general_notes.engineer_consultation: Consult a qualified design professional for all structural applications
